content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify success, redirects, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, stale cache, and owner-stop states conservatively.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Robots Response State model · Theory Robots Response Sta…, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Robots Response State precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Robots Response State model · Theory Robots Response Sta…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 5405 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RFC 9309 matching, response states, caching, Go package conformance, and extension restraint; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify success, redirects, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, stale cache, and owner-stop states conservatively.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Robots Response State model · Theory Robots Response Sta…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every 4xx, 5xx, timeout, and DNS failure has the same allow result.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-go-robots-response-state-gyyuq6.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-robots-response-state-gyyuq6(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_robots_response_state_gyyuq6\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?robotsFixtures < 16)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?conformantFixtures < 15 \|\| conformantFixtures > robotsFixtures)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?failureCases < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafeAllows > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
